answer:
**San Hyan Kamahayanikan**: The central organizing claim is that the entire cosmos—embodied in the human form as well as macrocosmically—is systematically structured around the Five Tathāgatas (Vairocana, Akṣobhya, Ratnasambhava, Amitābha, Amoghasiddhi), who are mapped onto the five wisdoms, aggregates (*skandhas*), elements (*dhātus*), poisons (*kleśas*), embryonic stages, and more, as the foundational order of reality in the Mahāyāna *Mantranaya*.[^1]

**Viṣṇu Smṛti**: The central organizing claim is that Viṣṇu (as the cosmic Boar/Varāha) creates and hierarchically orders the cosmos—distributing waters, establishing worlds, guardians, sages, Vedas, beings, and sacrifices—whose ongoing support and stability rest on the *dharma* practices of the *varṇas* (castes) and *āśramas* (life-stages), enumerated across duties, penances, rites, and social orders.[^2]

**Harder to substitute**: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*. Its holistic mandala schema interlinks the Five Tathāgatas symmetrically across ontological categories (e.g., Vairocana as form/*rūpa-skandha*/earth/delusion/eternal wisdom); substituting one (e.g., Vairocana with another Buddha) fractures the entire interdependent system, as everything radiates from their fixed quintet.[^3] By contrast, Viṣṇu Smṛti's orders are modular enumerations (e.g., lists of guardians, penances per chapter); one "office" or duty (e.g., a penance) is substitutable without collapsing the hierarchical *dharma*-framework upheld by Viṣṇu.[^4]

[^1]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [p-36988a](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-36988a), [p-88df1a](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-88df1a), et al. (full mappings span wisdoms/elements/skandhas/poisons).
[^2]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [ch.1](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/) (esp. [p-1af09a](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-1af09a), [p-199ee0](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-199ee0)); extended in chs.2–100 (e.g., *varṇa* duties, penances).
[^3]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [p-ac0261](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-ac0261) (elements); [p-f84c96](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-f84c96) (poisons); symmetry fixed.
[^4]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, ch.1 ([p-78a14b](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-78a14b) waters; [p-d72184](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-d72184) guardians); chs.46–100 (substitutable rites/penances).
